title: Negotiating With the Antichrist
content: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When rivals become mortal enemies, the instruments of politics and diplomacy cannot work.
snippet: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When
creator: Nina L. Khrushcheva
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Is Europe’s Economy Falling Short?
content: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance, a stagnant Europe is struggling to regain its place at the technological frontier.
snippet: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance,
creator: Philippe Aghion
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech’s Shadow Market Power
content: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale of these strategies, Silicon Valley’s dominance will remain unchallenged.
snippet: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale
creator: Helena Malikova
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The AI Revolution Mirrors the Green Transition
content: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an important role to play in guiding market forces on behalf of the public good.
snippet: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an
creator: Adam Michael Bauer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Where Is China’s Trillion-Dollar Trade Surplus Going?
content: Whereas China’s trade surplus used to be absorbed largely by the central bank and channeled into official reserves, it is now being redeployed by private actors in search of yield. This indicates China’s progress on financial deepening and underscores markets' increasingly central role in shaping the economy.
snippet: Whereas China’s trade surplus used to be absorbed largely by the central bank and channeled into official reserves, it is now being redeployed by private actors in search of yield. This indicates
creator: Miao Yanliang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Future of Development Finance Is Not Primarily About Money
content: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge than raising capital, which suggests that these institutions must rethink their operating model.
snippet: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge
creator: Tanu M. Goyal
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Nigeria’s Perilous French Gambit
content: Nigeria’s increasingly close relationship with France marks a departure from six decades of foreign policy aimed at reducing French influence in West Africa. An increasingly close relationship with a power that has long sought to undermine Nigerian influence in West Africa will benefit only politically connected business elites.
snippet: Nigeria’s increasingly close relationship with France marks a departure from six decades of foreign policy aimed at reducing French influence in West Africa. An increasingly close relationship with a
creator: Adekeye Adebajo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Autocracy Is Fueling Mexico’s Fiscal Deterioration
content: Mexico’s recent ratings downgrades are merely a symptom of a more serious malady: the ruling Morena party’s efforts to consolidate power permanently. To ensure the legal predictability foreign investors require, President Claudia Sheinbaum would need to confront Morena’s ideological legacy and, more importantly, its cartel ties.
snippet: Mexico’s recent ratings downgrades are merely a symptom of a more serious malady: the ruling Morena party’s efforts to consolidate power permanently. To ensure the legal predictability foreign
creator: Guillermo Ortiz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Trump Economy Is Bad News for Republicans
content: Donald Trump triumphed in the 2024 election by continually hammering on the Biden administration’s dismal inflation record. If his party gets shellacked in this year’s midterm elections, it will be because he has made Americans even worse off.
snippet: Donald Trump triumphed in the 2024 election by continually hammering on the Biden administration’s dismal inflation record. If his party gets shellacked in this year’s midterm elections, it will be
creator: Desmond Lachman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: SpaceX Is the New East India Company
content: Although SpaceX is not about to rule over foreign subjects, as the chartered companies founded in the early modern era did, it, too, is operating beyond the reach of any sovereign. And like its predecessors, it has already accumulated immense powers that governments will struggle to reclaim.
snippet: Although SpaceX is not about to rule over foreign subjects, as the chartered companies founded in the early modern era did, it, too, is operating beyond the reach of any sovereign. And like its
creator: Alessio Terzi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The ECB Should Not Raise Interest Rates Yet
content: Against a backdrop of acute uncertainty, weak growth, subdued wages, and elevated market interest rates, monetary tightening is warranted only when there is convincing evidence that higher inflation is becoming entrenched. No such evidence has yet materialized in Europe.
snippet: Against a backdrop of acute uncertainty, weak growth, subdued wages, and elevated market interest rates, monetary tightening is warranted only when there is convincing evidence that higher inflation
creator: Philippe Legrain
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: When Markets Run on Empty
content: Markets are flying high despite geopolitical turmoil because a broad-based willingness to keep spending has been matched by an ability to keep doing so. But as more economic participants exhaust their means and rely on debt, a painful moment of truth will arrive—sooner rather than later if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.
snippet: Markets are flying high despite geopolitical turmoil because a broad-based willingness to keep spending has been matched by an ability to keep doing so. But as more economic participants exhaust their
creator: Mohamed A. El-Erian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Pope and the AI Profiteers
content: In his first encyclical, Pope Leo XIV rightly rejects the idea that markets alone can be trusted to shape society’s technological future. Recognizing that AI raises questions that prices and profits cannot answer, the Chicago-born pope has posed a direct challenge to Chicago School economics.
snippet: In his first encyclical, Pope Leo XIV rightly rejects the idea that markets alone can be trusted to shape society’s technological future. Recognizing that AI raises questions that prices and profits
creator: Antara Haldar
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: AI Must Not Encroach on Human Dignity
content: In a world captivated by technological advances, Pope Leo XIV’s first encyclical seeks to shift attention away from the marvels and terrors of AI and toward the magnificence of humanity. The message that the technology lacks a “moral conscience” and is distinct from human cognition is necessary and welcome.

creator: Michael R. Strain
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Key Forces Now Shaping Markets and Geopolitics
content: Three major factors are likely to shape economic and geopolitical outcomes over the next several years: politically unconstrained AI development, a shift from the logic of globalization to zero-sum thinking, and heightened international tail risks. Much could go right, or at least as expected—until it doesn’t.
snippet: Three major factors are likely to shape economic and geopolitical outcomes over the next several years: politically unconstrained AI development, a shift from the logic of globalization to zero-sum
creator: Ian Bremmer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s Competitiveness Bogeyman
content: Calls for European leaders to protect domestic industry from "unfairly" subsidized Chinese competition have lately grown louder. But while tariffs might offer temporary relief to a few sectors, they cannot restore Europe's technological leadership, industrial dynamism, or export competitiveness.
snippet: Calls for European leaders to protect domestic industry from "unfairly" subsidized Chinese competition have lately grown louder. But while tariffs might offer temporary relief to a few sectors, they
creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Putting American Science on a MAGA Leash
content: A draft rule from US President Donald Trump’s White House Office of Management and Budget would subject all scientific research funding to the whims of political appointees, further undercutting a pillar of American power. The goal is to foreclose any future that is plural, contestable, and beyond the president’s control.
snippet: A draft rule from US President Donald Trump’s White House Office of Management and Budget would subject all scientific research funding to the whims of political appointees, further undercutting a
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Measles Came Roaring Back
content: Bangladesh is facing soaring measles cases not because of a single localized failure, but because of years of systemic erosion. Ultimately, maintaining herd immunity against highly contagious viruses requires relentless attention to supply and demand, as well as to the broader social and institutional foundations of public health.
snippet: Bangladesh is facing soaring measles cases not because of a single localized failure, but because of years of systemic erosion. Ultimately, maintaining herd immunity against highly contagious viruses
creator: M. Niaz Asadullah
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Pete Hegseth’s Dangerous Call to Arms in Asia
content: Much like it did in Europe, the Trump administration has called on US allies in the Indo-Pacific to increase military spending to 3.5% of GDP. But a defense buildup on this scale would jeopardize economic growth and regional unity, and could lead Southeast Asian countries to gravitate toward China’s strategic orbit.

creator: Thitinan Pongsudhirak
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Pope’s AI Vision and Its Limits
content: Pope Leo XIV’s encyclical on AI is clear-eyed about the limits of techno-utopianism. But its vision of technology directed toward human flourishing needs to be extended to include the interests of every sentient being, human and nonhuman, biological or—possibly—artificial.

creator: Peter Singer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is the US Stock Market Too Big?
content: With China and India having grown so fast as to compensate for the underperformance of their fellow BRICS (Brazil, Russia, and South Africa), the center of gravity in the global economy continues to shift eastward. The question now is whether—and when—equity valuations will reflect this underlying reality.

creator: Jim O'Neill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Brazil’s Bad Vibes
content: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem for the current government; but winning the news cycle is not a real solution.
snippet: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem
creator: Laura Carvalho
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Living Ghosts of Violent Radicalism
content: Following World War II, the most militant student movements emerged in the former Axis powers as a way to cope with previous generations' complicity. But while the Red Army Faction and others were haunted by memories of past crimes, today's young people have mostly forgotten, and a growing number think far-right politics are cool.

creator: Ian Buruma
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Using AI to Test Policy Language
content: Policymakers have long understood that language affects economic behavior, but until recently, there were few tools capable of systematically analyzing its effects. AI may now help close that gap, enabling officials to anticipate how framing and timing can move markets and shift public opinion.
